title: CustomNamedValues Object
description: A collection of named values. The values are strings that Fusion stores but can be anything you choose. If you have several things you need to save you can choose to combine the data into a JSON or XML representation and save it as a single custom value or create a new custom value or each unique value you want to store. Fusion doesn't care what the value is or what it represents but only saves and provides access to it.

methods.name: addOrSetValue
methods.description: Adds or updates a value. If the specified ID does not exist, a new named value is added. If the ID does exist, the named value is updated with the specified value.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: idByIndex
methods.description: Function that returns the name of a value specified by its index.

methods.name: isExistingValue
methods.description: Function that returns if a value with the specified ID exists or not.

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Removes the specified value from the collection.

methods.name: value
methods.description: Function that returns the specified value given its ID.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of values in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

addOrSetValue.title: CustomNamedValues.addOrSetValue Method
addOrSetValue.description: Adds or updates a value. If the specified ID does not exist, a new named value is added. If the ID does exist, the named value is updated with the specified value.
addOrSetValue.return_description: Returns true is successful and false if it failed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the value to create or change.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The string to assign to the value.

classType.title: CustomNamedValues.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

idByIndex.title: CustomNamedValues.idByIndex Method
idByIndex.description: Function that returns the name of a value specified by its index.
idByIndex.return_description: Returns the ID of the specified item or asserts if an out of bounds index is used.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return the name of. The first item in the collection has an index of 0 and the last item is the count of this collection minus 1.

isExistingValue.title: CustomNamedValues.isExistingValue Method
isExistingValue.description: Function that returns if a value with the specified ID exists or not.
isExistingValue.return_description: Returns true if a value with the ID exists.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the value to check if it exists.

remove.title: CustomNamedValues.remove Method
remove.description: Removes the specified value from the collection.
remove.return_description: Returns true if the value was successfully removed and false if it failed. Failure is typically because the specified ID does not exist within the collection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the value to remove.

value.title: CustomNamedValues.value Method
value.description: Function that returns the specified value given its ID.
value.return_description: Returns the value or an empty string if the specified ID was not found.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the value, which was assigned when the value was created.

count.title: CustomNamedValues.count Property
count.description: The number of values in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = customNamedValues_var.count

isValid.title: CustomNamedValues.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = customNamedValues_var.isValid

objectType.title: CustomNamedValues.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = customNamedValues_var.objectType
